exploration_log.aha_moment: The static dictionaries cache wrapper instances keyed by Type. When a new IServiceProvider is built in tests, the old cached wrappers still reference types from previous builds. Adding ClearCache() and calling it on AddMediatR is the minimal fix.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have jumped directly to the two edit sites without reading the full Mediator.cs first.

alternatives: Make the dictionaries instance-level (larger change, potentially performance impact)
key_decisions.decision: Add ClearCache() static method and call it in AddMediatR
key_decisions.reasoning: The task explicitly offers both options and the static method approach is less disruptive. Making dictionaries instance-level would require threading through the Mediator instance everywhere, which is a larger refactor.

search_sequence.reasoning: Need to see the static dictionary field declarations to understand what ClearCache() must clear
Task says to call ClearCache during AddMediatR registration
search_sequence.result: Found AddMediatR(IServiceCollection, MediatRServiceConfiguration) as the place to add the call

solve_notes: Added a `ClearCache()` static method to `Mediator` that calls `.Clear()` on all three static `ConcurrentDictionary` caches. Called `Mediator.ClearCache()` at the end of `MediatRServiceCollectionExtensions.AddMediatR` so each DI container registration starts with a fresh cache, preventing stale wrapper references from prior test runs.
task_text:
Fix static ConcurrentDictionary caches in Mediator causing memory leaks in test scenarios

In `src/MediatR/Mediator.cs`, `_requestHandlers`, `_notificationHandlers`,
and `_streamRequestHandlers` are `static readonly ConcurrentDictionary`
fields. In test scenarios where `IServiceProvider` is rebuilt per test,
the cached wrapper instances hold references to stale type metadata.
Fix by making the dictionaries instance-level or adding a `ClearCache()`
static method, and update `MediatRServiceCollectionExtensions` to call
it during registration.

test_selection.coverage_skip_reason: Auditor could not generate baseline coverage — see auditor report

thrash_preventing_defs.name: _requestHandlers
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: verifying the static ConcurrentDictionary field declarations confirms what ClearCache() must clear and that they are all in one class

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is the two edited methods. The thrash-preventing set adds the static field declarations so an AI agent can enumerate all three dictionary fields upfront without searching through the class. Without this, the agent might only clear one or two of the three dictionaries.
